fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 65 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False and skipped when `clamp` is True. It should be `if clamp:` not `if not clamp:`.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should be entered, but the inverted condition skips it — check that clamping is applied iff clamp is True
The then-branch of the if should only execute when clamp is False (i.e., the condition should be `if clamp`); if we are inside the branch and clamp is True, the condition is inverted

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the final y value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; if the condition is inverted, clamping is skipped and this will be violated
lo__1 and hi__1 are computed inside the branch; they should only be computed (and used) when clamp is True, so reaching this point implies clamp must be True
